acceptOwnership().details: The new owner accepts the ownership transfer.

allow(bytes32,address).details: The caller must not be in the deny list and must be allowed to use `handle` for allow() to succeed. If not, allow() reverts.

allowForDecryption(bytes32[]).details: The caller must not be in the deny list and must be allowed to use `handlesList[i]` for allowForDecryption() to succeed.      If not, allowForDecryption() reverts.

allowTransient(bytes32,address).details: The caller must not be in the deny list and must be allowed to use `handle` for allowTransient() to succeed.      If not, allowTransient() reverts. The Coprocessor contract can always `allowTransient`,      contrarily to `allow`.

returns._0: isAllowedTransient Whether the account can access transiently the handle.

cleanTransientStorage().details: This function removes the transient allowances, which could be useful for integration with Account Abstraction when bundling several UserOps calling the FHEVMExecutor Coprocessor.

params.contractAddress: The contract address to delegate access to.
params.delegate: The address of the account that receives the delegation.
params.expirationDate: The UNIX timestamp when the user decryption delegation expires.

returns._0: pauserSetAddress Address of the PauserSet contract.

params.contractAddress: The contract address to delegate access to.
params.delegate: The address of the account that receives the delegation.
params.delegator: The address of the account that delegates access to its handles.

returns._0: expirationDate The UNIX timestamp when the user decryption delegation expires (0 means delegation is inactive).
string Name and the version of the contract.
isAccountDenied Whether the account is on the deny list.
isAllowed Whether the account can access the handle.
isAllowed Whether the handle is allowed for decryption.

params.contractAddress: The contract address to delegate access to.
params.delegate: The address of the account that receives the delegation.
params.delegator: The address of the account that delegates access to its handles.
params.handle: The handle to check for delegated user decryption.

returns._0: isDelegatedForUserDecryption Whether the handle can be accessed for delegated user decryption.

multicall(bytes[]).details: Receives and executes a batch of function calls on this contract.

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

pause().details: Triggers stopped state. Only a pauser address can pause. The contract must not be paused.

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

pendingOwner().details: Returns the address of the pending owner.

returns._0: isAllowed Whether the account can access the handle.

proxiableUUID().details: Implementation of the ERC-1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

params.contractAddress: The contract address to delegate access to.
params.delegate: The address of the account that receives the delegation.

transferOwnership(address).details: Starts the ownership transfer of the contract to a new account. Replaces the pending transfer if there is one. Can only be called by the current owner. Setting `newOwner` to the zero address is allowed; this can be used to cancel an initiated ownership transfer.

unpause().details: Returns to normal state. Only owner can unpause. The contract must be paused.

upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

allow(bytes32,address).notice: Allows the use of `handle` for the address `account`.

allowForDecryption(bytes32[]).notice: Allows a list of handles to be decrypted.

allowTransient(bytes32,address).notice: Allows the use of `handle` by address `account` for this transaction.

allowedTransient(bytes32,address).notice: Checks whether the account is allowed to use the handle in the same transaction (transient).

delegateForUserDecryption(address,addres.notice: Delegates an account the access to handles for user decryption, for instance, in the context of account abstraction for issuing user decryption requests from a smart contract account.

getFHEVMExecutorAddress().notice: Getter function for the FHEVMExecutor contract address.

getPauserSetAddress().notice: Getter function for the PauserSet contract address.

getUserDecryptionDelegationExpirationDat.notice: Get the expiration date of a delegation for user decryption.

getVersion().notice: Getter for the name and version of the contract.

isAccountDenied(address).notice: Returns `true` if `account` is deny-listed and `false` otherwise.

isAllowed(bytes32,address).notice: Returns whether the account is allowed to use the `handle`, either due to allowTransient() or allow().

isAllowedForDecryption(bytes32).notice: Checks whether a handle is allowed for decryption.

isHandleDelegatedForUserDecryption(addre.notice: Returns whether an account is delegated to access the handle for user decryption.

isPauser(address).notice: Returns wether specified account is in the set of pausers.

persistAllowed(bytes32,address).notice: Returns `true` if address `a` is allowed to use `c` and `false` otherwise.

revokeDelegationForUserDecryption(addres.notice: Revokes the access to handles for user decryption delegated to an account.
